outcome_text: Internal experts who did not serve as combatants of the system and/or independent assessors are involved in regular assessments and updates. Domain experts, users, AI actors external to the team that developed or deployed the AI system, and affected communities are consulted in support of assessments as necessary per organizational risk tolerance.
scoring_text: Knowledge of independent AI assessment practices, including engaging internal experts outside the development team, coordinating independent assessor reviews, consulting domain experts and affected communities, and structuring assessment involvement according to organizational risk tolerance levels.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of independent AI assessment practices, including engaging internal experts outside the development team, coordinating independent assessor reviews, consulting domain experts and affected communities, and structuring assessment involvement according to organizational risk tolerance levels.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in conducting AI impact assessments, including risk tiering, harm identification, bias evaluation, human oversight documentation, and residual risk determination.
comments: NIST AI RMF MS-1.3 (MEASURE) equal WIDAI AG domain. AG-S-005 is the strongest match (STS: 0.677, cosine: 0.697). Secondary: AG-T-010 (AG, cosine: 0.684), LS-S-006 (LS, cosine: 0.674).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the risk management outcome) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.8/10 (raw 0.677). Shared vocabulary: [ai, risk]. NIST AI RMF competency implication terms [according, affected, assessment, assessor, communities, consulting] vs WIDAI terms [assessments, bias, conducting, determination, documentation, evaluation] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.877 (P=0.865, R=0.888). R exceeds P by 0.024 — the WIDAI KSA's 20 words are well-captured by the competency implication's 34 words, but the competency implication contains broader scope.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: notable entailment (98.8%). "Knowledge of independent AI assessment practices, including engaging internal ex" shows logical implication toward "Skill in conducting AI impact assessments, including risk tiering, harm identifi" — stronger than typical for cross-framework competency pairs, reinforcing the STS-based relationship.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.697 ≈ STS 0.677 (gap 0.021). Independent encoding and cross-attention agree. Shared terms [ai, risk] are sufficient for independent encoding to detect the match.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 8.9%. Shared tokens: [ai, risk]. NIST AI RMF-only: [according, affected, assessment, assessor, communities, consulting]. WIDAI-only: [assessments, bias, conducting, determination, documentation, evaluation].
